chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. D – 03 of 2026 (Imran Khan versus The State) Before: Mr. Mahmood A. Khan, J. Mr. Khalid Hussain Shahani, J. Date of hearing: 17.02.2026 Date of decision: 17.02.2026 Mr. Gul Hassan Lashari, Advocate along with applicant (present on interim pre-arrest bail). Syed Sardar Ali Shah Rizvi, Additional Prosecutor General. O R D E R Mahmood A. Khan, J. – Applicant seeks bail in Crime No.14 of 2025, registered at Police Station Babarloi, District Khairpur, under Section 9(c) of CNS Act. 2. Learned Counsel for the applicant submits copy of order dated 14.01.2026, as passed by this Court wherein one of us was the member, whereby the main accused alleged in the matter has already been granted bail, and further contends that against the present applicant the ownership of the subject vehicle is alleged, whereas the applicant had sold the vehicle and gone out of country, whereafter on having come to know that his CNIC has been blocked, he has come back to face the trial, and that he was not nominated in the FIR. 3. Learned Additional Prosecutor General, however, severely opposes the bail contending that the present applicant is an out-of-province person and further heinous crime is involved. 4. Having heard the learned Counsels and gone through the record, the main accused already granted bail, the present applicant not named in the FIR stands at better footing. Accordingly, concession of bail is extended to him. The interim pre-arrest bail already granted to the Cr. Bail App. No. D – 03 of 2026 Page 2 of 2 applicant stands confirmed on same terms and conditions. However, the surety amount of Rs.30,000/- contained in order dated 06.01.2026 is enhanced to the amount of Rs.1,00,000/-.

D – 03 of 2026 Page 2 of 2 applicant stands confirmed on same terms and conditions. However, the surety amount of Rs.30,000/- contained in order dated 06.01.2026 is enhanced to the amount of Rs.1,00,000/-. The bail application stands allowed in the above terms. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
